methods.name: zonevu.Services.WellService.WellService.update_well
zonevu.Services.WellService.WellService.copy_well
documentation:
This function makes and saves a copy of a well and all of its child data.
\:param well: the well to be copied
\:param well_copy_name: name of copied well
\:param well_copy_number: number of copied well. If None, the well number from the source well will be used.
\:param well_copy_uwi: UWI of copied well
\:return: the copy of the well

NOTES:
1- we are copying a well and all of its constituent child data, including surveys, well logs, tops, etc. We will do

> a "Phase I" copy of all of this, except the geosteering interpretations.

2- we will also copy the geosteering interpretations -- this will be "Phase II".

: This is a special case because these refer to other wells, namely, type wells.
  We will assume that those wells are not being copied, therefore the references in the
  geosteering interpretations to those wells will remain valid.

3- The geosteering interpretations also refer to LWD well log curves on this well that is being copied. Those

: references need to be updated since we have copied in Phase I the well logs on this very well.

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.calc_percent_in_zone
documentation:
Computes the percent in zone for a wellbore (that is, a deviation survey) for a geosteering interpretation
\:param interp:
\:param stations: a list of survey stations that defines the wellbore that was geosteered
\:return:

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.make_evenly_spaced_picks
documentation:
Converts the actual picks in a geosteering interpretation into evenly spaced picks, with no faults.
\:param interp: A geosteering interpretation
\:param interval: The sample interval in MD for output picks
\:param first_md: Beginning MD for output picks
\:param last_md: Ending MD for output picks
\:return:
Note: faults are discarded.

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.create_extended_picks
documentation:
Flattens the data in a geosteering interpretation into a list of extended geosteering picks
\:param interp: A geosteering interpretation
\:param strat_cols: The stratigraphic columns of the well that was geosteered
\:param picks: Picks to extend. If None, will extend the picks on the interpretation
\:return:

Note: Each pick will have a list of horizon depths for all horizons in the interpretation.

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.make_blocks_and_faults
documentation:
Computes the blocks and faults for a geosteering interpretation
\:param interp: A geosteering interpretation
\:param interval: If provided, blocks will be of width 'interval' and no faults will be generated.
\:return: a list of geosteering blocks and faults

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.get_block_type_curve_info
documentation:
Find information about the type curve used on a geosteering block.
\:param interp: The geosteering interpretation that this block is from
\:param block: The block in question
\:return:

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.make_contiguous_blocks
documentation:
Computes a "blocks only" set of blocks of the interpretation, where blocks are contiguous in MD (any gaps filled).
\:param interp: a geosteering interpretation
\:return: a list of geosteering blocks that are contiguous with no faults

functions.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geosteering.Calcs.create_pseudo_welllog
documentation:
Convert the picks and horizons of a geosteering interpretation into a well log data structure.
\:param zonevu: ZoneVu client instance
\:param interp: A geosteering interpretation
\:param strat_col: The strat column referenced by the geosteering interpretation
\:param interval: The sample interval in MD for horizon data points
\:param first_md: Beginning MD for horizon data points
\:param last_md: Ending MD for horizon data points
\:return: A "pseudo" well log and well log curves.

Note: A well log data structure might be a convenient data structure of a geosteering interpretation for some
: applications.

  : Each pseudo curve will be one horizon from the geosteering interpretation with its curve mnemonic

  set to the formation symbol of that horizon.

  : The index_curve_id of the well log will be set to the curve index of the target horizon.

methods.name: zonevu.DataModels.Geospatial.GridGeometry.GridGeometry.from_simple_grid
documentation:
Method to set up a simple grid geometry.
For inclination = 0, rows are west to east, and columns south to north
Must provide origin, dx, and dy in distance units of provided CRS
@param grid: a simple grid definition
@return: a partially filled GridGeometry, where geolocations and geo-inclination not populated.

NOTES:
We set up here a CW grid geometry, where rows (inlines) run from East to West, and columns (crosslines)
run South to North.
